channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I stopped treating AI memory as summaries. I now think in handoffs.
item.description: In my last post, I wrote that AI memory should be treated as product state, not as a prompt trick. After publishing that post, I kept running into the next problem while working on the memory...

item.title: Alphabetic Array Sort in JavaScript
item.description: 1. Array sort() The sort() method sorts the elements of an array. Syntax array.sort(compareFunction) The compareFunction determines the sorting order. Sorting Strings When no compare function is...

item.title: Netris raises $15M Series A from a16z to help AI neoclouds go live faster
item.description: Netris provides software that runs on network switches, and offers a platform that helps neocloud operators reduce the time it takes to go live.

item.title: Let your agent negotiate meeting times over email
item.description: Most "AI scheduling assistant" demos cheat. They show a model that already knows the time, calls "create event" once, and takes a bow. That's not scheduling — that's data entry with a chat...

item.description: AI Agent Memory in 2026: How It Works and When to Use It Most agent demos forget everything between calls. That works for toy scripts. It breaks the moment you want an agent that improves over a...

item.title: The reinstall bug almost every Flutter app ships
item.description: You can't see it in the simulator. Your testers won't hit it. Your crash reporter won't catch it — because it isn't a crash. It's a returning user quietly losing their app. The setup. Your app...

item.title: Repositioning retail for the AI era
item.description: Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping retail, but not in the ways consumers might immediately notice. The biggest transformation may not be flashy virtual try-ons or chatbot shopping...

item.title: Chrome Ad Blocker with 10M+ Installs Found with Dormant Script Injection Capability
item.description: An analysis of a popular Google Chrome ad block extension for YouTube has uncovered the ability to execute arbitrary JavaScript code. According to Island, the extension, named Adblock for YouTube...

item.title: 2 days left to save up to $190: Join 1,000+ founders and investors at TechCrunch Founder Summit
item.description: 2 days left to lock in your spot at TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 and save up to $190 before Early Bird rates expire on June 26 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here.

item.title: EverQuest Legends is a powerful nostalgia machine
item.description: I wasn't surprised when I got the call that my dad was dying, even though we'd been estranged for many years. He'd suffered addiction for decades and eventually ran out of time, which also meant...

item.title: Samsung’s new budget phone costs $50 more despite downgrades
item.description: The Galaxy A27 has been announced, and at $349.99 it's $50 more than last year's A26. That's understandable in the current economic climate, but a harder sell given that Samsung has also...

item.title: Adobe acquires image and video enhancement tool maker Topaz Labs
item.description: Adobe said that it will integrate Topaz Labs' tools across its apps.

item.title: Leica’s $6,690 SL3-P pairs 44-megapixel stills with 8K video
item.description: Following the launch of the SL3 in 2024 and last year's SL3-S that was optimized for speed and capturing fast moving subjects, Leica announced the new SL3-P today delivering some of the best...

item.title: Apple raises prices on Macs, iPads, and more by hundreds of dollars
item.description: Apple is hiking the prices of its MacBooks, iPads, and other devices by hundreds of dollars in response to the ongoing memory and storage shortages. While the new MacBook Neo will now start at...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Smart TV Proxyware, 24-Year curl Bug, AI Crime Forums + 13 More Stories
item.description: It’s dumb out there again. This week has the usual smell of prod on fire and nobody wanting to admit who left the door open — old creds still working, trusted apps doing sketchy crap, browser...

item.title: The Download: Europe’s heat wave hits the grid, and IBM’s chip targets Moore’s Law
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants Europe...

item.title: Amazon ups India bet with fresh $13B AI infrastructure investment
item.description: Amazon’s latest India investment comes as global tech companies race to expand AI infrastructure in the country.

item.title: Ford had to hire back former engineers to fix mistakes made by its automated systems

item.title: Interesting Paper Exploring Prompt Injection
item.description: This is a fascinating explotation of how LLMs fall for prompt injection attacks. It turns out that they learn to recognize the style of text in different role/instruction blocks, and not just the...

item.title: Surviving the Mythos Era: Richard Bejtlich on the Case for NDR

item.title: World Cup Teams Are in a Race for AI Dominance
item.description: This year, FIFA is providing an AI agent that any team can use. Is it enough to level the playing field or will future winners be determined by which team can afford the best tools?

item.title: Disney agrees to pay $50 million to YouTube TV and DirecTV subscribers
item.description: YouTube TV and DirecTV Stream customers may be eligible for a cash payout, after Disney agreed to pay $50 million to settle claims that it forced the services to increase their subscription...
